description_long: This is a classic submachine gun with a robust, dark metallic body and distinct wooden furniture, including a fixed stock and a vertical foregrip. The weapon features a large, round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of the Thompson submachine gun. The barrel is ribbed for cooling and ends with a slotted muzzle device, likely a compensator or muzzle brake. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the receiver for aiming. The trigger guard and pistol grip are integrated into the main body, with the wooden stock extending rearward for shoulder support. The overall design is iconic, evoking early to mid-20th-century military and law enforcement use.
description: A dark metal submachine gun with wooden stock and grips, featuring a drum magazine and ribbed barrel. Classic Thompson-style design with iron sights and a slotted muzzle device.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine and the ribbed barrel aligned directly toward the viewer.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the wooden stock, vertical foregrip, drum magazine, and ribbed barrel with slotted muzzle device.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the back of the drum magazine, with the barrel receding away.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same key features as view 1, with clear visibility of the wooden elements and iron sights.
